nodes.label: people | perish | save
nodes.sentence: Once upon a time, in a quiet Germanic realm cloaked by endless forests and misty moors, all the people had perished save for two helpless children—a baby boy and his elder sister.

nodes.label: parent | die | ∅
nodes.sentence: Their parents had died while they slept, leaving the siblings to awaken alone in a small timbered hut by the edge of the Schwarzwald.

nodes.label: girl | be | first
nodes.sentence: The girl, a calm and watchful child, was the first to rouse.

nodes.label: she | look | ∅
nodes.sentence: She looked around and, seeing no one but her little brother smiling in his dreams, lay back down in the straw-filled bed.

nodes.label: day | pass | ∅
nodes.sentence: Ten days passed, and the boy shifted slightly without opening his eyes.

nodes.label: radiance | hover | watch
nodes.sentence: Yet in those quiet watches, a faint, silvery radiance hovered about his head, lighting the dark hut with an otherworldly glow that puzzled his sister.

nodes.label: growth | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: As the seasons turned, the girl grew into a young woman; meanwhile, the boy’s growth was slow and measured.

nodes.label: year | go | youth
nodes.sentence: Many years went by before he could crawl, and well into his youth before he could stand unaided.

nodes.label: sister | craft | bow
nodes.sentence: When he finally learned to walk, his sister crafted a small bow and arrow for him, and hung a tiny snail shell around his neck, softly saying, “Your name shall be Schneckenkind—He of the Little Shell.”

nodes.label: boy | venture | wood
nodes.sentence: Every day, the boy ventured into the dense woods with his bow, aiming at the small creatures of the forest.

nodes.label: bird | be | tit
nodes.sentence: The first bird he felled was a lively coal tit.

nodes.label: sister | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: His sister was delighted and carefully preserved the delicate creature as a trophy.

nodes.label: he | catch | squirrel
nodes.sentence: On the next day, he caught a red squirrel, which she also saved.

nodes.label: partridge | provide | meal
nodes.sentence: By the third day, a wood partridge provided the evening meal.

nodes.label: schneckenkind | roam | courage
nodes.sentence: With newfound courage, Schneckenkind roamed further into the forest.

nodes.label: skill | grow | ∅
nodes.sentence: His skill grew as he hunted deer, boars, and even bears.

nodes.label: he | become | woodsman
nodes.sentence: Though small, he became a master woodsman, bringing back all he caught to share with his sister.

nodes.label: light | dance | head
nodes.sentence: Each time he entered their lodge, that same mysterious light danced about his head, filling the room with a strange, silver splendor.

nodes.label: he | remain | ∅
nodes.sentence: Though Schneckenkind had reached the age of a man in years, he remained as small as a child.

nodes.label: he | spy | figure
nodes.sentence: One winter’s day, wandering near a frozen lake, he spied a giant figure busy trapping beavers on the ice.

nodes.label: giant | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The giant was broad-shouldered, clad in a heavy fur coat lined with wolf pelts, and wielded a massive spear.

nodes.label: schneckenkind | creep | ∅
nodes.sentence: After the giant had hunted many beavers and hauled them onto a wooden sled, Schneckenkind crept alongside and, wielding his enchanted shell, deftly snipped off the tail of one beaver before slipping away.

nodes.label: he | baffle | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the giant returned, he was baffled to find one of his catches missing its tail, muttering curses in his guttural voice.

nodes.label: schneckenkind | repeat | feat
nodes.sentence: The next day, Schneckenkind repeated the feat, again stealing a beaver’s tail from under the giant’s nose.

nodes.label: giant | swear | ∅
nodes.sentence: Furious, the giant swore he would find and punish the elusive thief.

nodes.label: magic | make | ∅
nodes.sentence: Yet, despite his keen eyes and heavy footsteps, he found no trace—Schneckenkind’s magic made him invisible and silent as the forest shadows.

nodes.label: giant | arrive | day
nodes.sentence: On the following day, the giant arrived early, hoping to trap the little man.

nodes.label: he | chase | they
nodes.sentence: Hunting the fresh tracks, he chased them through snow and ice, but just as he neared his hut, he caught sight of Schneckenkind—who allowed himself to be seen.

nodes.label: giant | growl | ∅
nodes.sentence: The giant growled thunderously, “Who are you, little man?

nodes.label: i | mean | ∅
nodes.sentence: I mean to vanquish you.”

nodes.label: schneckenkind | reply | bravery
nodes.sentence: But Schneckenkind replied with calm bravery, “You will never catch me.”

nodes.label: giant | lunge | ∅
nodes.sentence: The giant lunged, but Schnneckenkind vanished like mist.

nodes.label: ∅ | demand | giant
nodes.sentence: “Where are you now?” demanded the giant.

nodes.label: voice | come | belt
nodes.sentence: “Under your belt,” came the voice.

nodes.label: giant | strike | hand
nodes.sentence: The giant struck his broad hand down, but found only empty air.

nodes.label: giant | roar | ∅
nodes.sentence: the giant roared.

nodes.label: voice | tease | ∅
nodes.sentence: the voice teased.

nodes.label: giant | grab | nose
nodes.sentence: The giant grabbed at his nose, only to hear the tiny voice laughing far away on the snowy ground.

nodes.label: ∅ | count | tail
nodes.sentence: Count your beaver tails—and know that I have taken one for my sister, too.”

nodes.label: light | return | ∅
nodes.sentence: As he vanished, the silver light returned, bathing the snow in a magical glow, a sight the slow-witted giant could not comprehend.

nodes.label: schneckenkind | tell | sister
nodes.sentence: Returning home, Schneckenkind told his sister that their time together was nearing its end.

nodes.label: it | be | fate
nodes.sentence: “I must journey away; it is my fate.

nodes.label: she | say | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Tell me, where would you wish to dwell?”

“My heart longs for the place where dawn breaks—the eastern sky,” she said softly.

nodes.label: ∅ | think | i
nodes.sentence: When you see the painted skies, think of me, shining bright.”

nodes.label: ∅ | make | home
nodes.sentence: “And I,” said Schneckenkind, “will make my home on the highest mountains, where streams run clear and the air is pure.

nodes.label: light | shine | head
nodes.sentence: There, golden light will shine forever around my head, and they will call me Berggeist—the Little Wild Man of the Mountains.

nodes.label: i | seek | spirit
nodes.sentence: But before we part, I must seek out the spirits who rule the earth and learn who will be friend or foe.”

nodes.label: he | travel | land
nodes.sentence: So he traveled far across the land and even descended into the deep earth, where a fierce giant spirit kept a boiling cauldron.

nodes.label: giant | recognize | schneckenkind
nodes.sentence: This giant, kin to the one who hunted beavers, recognized Schneckenkind and cast him into the kettle to drown.

nodes.label: schneckenkind | empty | cauldron
nodes.sentence: But with the power of his magic shell, Schneckenkind emptied the cauldron in an instant and leapt free, unharmed.

nodes.label: he | return | sister
nodes.sentence: He returned to his sister and told her all he had seen.

nodes.label: ∅ | dwell | spirit
nodes.sentence: “At each of the four corners of the earth dwells a spirit, and above them all a Great Being who decides our fate.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | spirit
nodes.sentence: There is also a wicked spirit deep beneath the earth to avoid.

nodes.label: you | carry | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the winds blow from the four corners, you must go—you will be carried to where you long to be.

nodes.label: i | dwell | height
nodes.sentence:
I shall dwell among the rocky heights, where my kin delight.”

With that, Schneckenkind took his bow and set out up a towering mountain.

nodes.label: ∅ | blow | ∅
nodes.sentence: Blow, winds, blow!

nodes.label: sister | linger | ∅
nodes.sentence:
My sister lingers 
Where the dawn with rosy fingers 
Paints the skies on morning’s breath, 
Bathing clouds in shades of death.

nodes.label: ∅ | guide | wood
nodes.sentence:
There her gleaming face I’ll see, 
Guiding through the wood and lea.

nodes.label: ∅ | sing | wild
nodes.sentence:
While I roam the mountain peaks, 
Sing my wild and wordless speaks.

nodes.label: wind | rise | ∅
nodes.sentence: Soon the winds rose, swirling through the forest, lifting his sister toward the blush of dawn.

nodes.label: she | remain | light
nodes.sentence: There she remains still, a shining light in the morning sky—the Morning Star of the Schwarzwald, forever watching.
